repository.name: Depth anything 3-File Content
repository.type: research-oriented Python package for monocular and multi-view 3D inference

overview.purpose: Packages Depth Anything 3 as a reusable Python API, CLI, Gradio UI, backend service, and long-video streaming pipeline for estimating depth, pose, and downstream 3D assets.
overview.shape: Layered package architecture with a dense model and utility core, multiple operator-facing interfaces, and a separate streaming subsystem for long-sequence processing.

entry_points.role: Typer commands for image, video, COLMAP, backend, and Gradio workflows
Interactive web UI for reconstruction workflows
Resident model backend for queued GPU inference
Chunked long-video inference with loop-aware alignment

modules.role: Core model stack including DA3, DINOv2, DPT-style heads, camera encoders, and Gaussian splat adapters.
Shared geometry, export, I/O, visualization, memory, and model-loading helpers used across interfaces.
Execution layer for local inference, backend orchestration, and gallery handling.
UI-specific handlers, visualization helpers, and wiring for the Gradio app.
Loop detection, Sim3 alignment, and acceleration helpers for long-sequence reconstruction.
Operational documentation for CLI and API usage.

runtime: Hugging Face model artifacts or local checkpoints

observations: The repo offers the same core inference capability through API, CLI, Gradio, and backend-service entry points instead of a single operator surface.
Most implementation weight sits in model and utility layers, which is typical of a research-to-product bridge codebase.
Export helpers for GLB, COLMAP, NPZ, depth visualization, and Gaussian splats show the project targets downstream 3D workflows, not just raw depth maps.
The `da3_streaming` subtree acts like a specialized extension rather than a first-class package sibling.
unknowns: The analyzed folder contains a prepared repository snapshot, not the raw source tree, so conclusions depend on the `preprep-repo` capture quality.
Test coverage and CI behavior are not visible in the snapshot metadata.
Exact pretrained weight variants, checkpoint management strategy, and deployment-scale performance expectations are only partially documented.
